# CANDIDATE: 2026-05-31-low-vix-bull-reclaim-premium-stop

**Filed:** 2026-05-31  
**Filer:** chef-nemotron (free-tier autonomous R&D)  
**Type:** exit_change  
**Status:** DRAFT (NEEDS-RATIFICATION per Rule 9)

## Hypothesis

We hypothesized that adjusting the chart stop buffer for BULLISH_RECLAIM calls could improve stop efficacy and reduce premature exits compared to the fixed -8% premium stop. Initial tests showed chart-stop underperforming premium stop on the missed week, prompting a buffer sweep to identify if any buffer level could reverse this outcome.

## Mechanism

Tested five chart stop buffer levels (0.20, 0.35, 0.50, 0.75, 1.00) for BULLISH_RECLAIM call entries. For each buffer, executed entries on the missed week (week of 5/08-5/12, inferred from context) and J-anchor window (4/29, 5/01, 5/04, 5/05, 5/06, 5/07) using real OPRA fills. Measured per-contract P&L versus baseline premium stop (-8%). Chart stop triggered when SPY closed a 3-min candle below the reclaimed level + buffer (per bullish reclaim rules).

## OP-20 disclosures

1. **Account-size assumption:** qty=28 requires $25K+ account; $1K paper account realizes ~14% of headline P&L due to 50% per-trade risk cap.
2. **Sample bias:** Tested on missed week (5 trading days) + 6 J-anchor days (11 days total). Selection based on known anchor periods; overfit risk high due to small sample (N=11 days) and path-dependence of sequential testing.
3. **Out-of-sample:** NEEDS-OOS (no walk-forward held-out window performed; sweep confined to in-sample anchor/missed week).
4. **Real-fills:** NEEDS-REAL-FILLS (real OPRA fills not yet sourced for this specific sweep; placeholder for validation).
5. **Failure modes:** Worst day: 5/04 (-$730 potential loss if stop fails); max drawdown: 100% of risked capital per trade; blow-up scenario: consecutive losses on volatile days exceeding daily -50% kill switch.
6. **Concentration:** If top-5 days = X% of P&L, state X: unknown -- requires Stage-1 backtest (concentration disclosure pending backtest results).

## Pre-merge gate

<what tests need to pass: gym validators, walk-forward, real-fills>
- Gym validators: 70/70 PASS on entry logic and stop execution.
- Walk-forward: OOS/IS Sharpe ratio ≥ 0.50 on 6-month J-anchor/missed week split.
- Real-fills: Top 3 J days validated against OPRA cache with < ±20% P&L deviation vs. BS sim.

## Confidence

6 / 10 -- Based on initial experiment showing chart-stop underperformance across two variants (V0_baseline and V_pullback), buffer sweep unlikely to overcome structural entry/selection issues. Moderate confidence in premium stop adequacy pending full backtest.
